concerns.analysis: The source document contains a DEQ (Department of Environmental Quality) agency logo in the bottom left corner of the first page. This is a meaningful organizational logo. The HTML output does not contain any image corresponding to this logo.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: DEQ logo in the bottom left corner of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: Missing element for the DEQ logo.

concerns.analysis: The source document has a numbered list of requirements on the first page. In the HTML output, this list is correctly extracted as structured HTML (<ol>). However, an image is also inserted immediately following the list's closing paragraph, which based on its alt text ('A snippet of an official document containing a numbered list of requirements regarding sediment basin baffles and contacting well') is a screenshot/crop of the text itself. This results in the user seeing the same text content t
concerns.output_quote:
1. Provide proof...
    2. ...
  2. Please re-contact...
followed by

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 496 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=496. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.5x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.50. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

The source document features underlines for emphasis on phrases like 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' on page 1, and 'Certified Mail' on page 2. The extraction output drops this emphasis entirely, presenting them as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined text: "Certified Mail" and "Return Receipt Requested"

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
